If you printed it out, it might be 10 pages or even longer. There was a headline at the top and the standard formula used was problem agitate solve. The idea was that you grabbed your prospects attention with a headline, then you'd find some kind of problem that they had in their lives. They bought a guitar, but they didn't know how to play songs.

## [18:06 - 21:08]

Or they didn't have a girlfriend. Or their dog wouldn't come when it was called. So you define the problem, then you get them agitated about that problem. You make them feel even worse about their problem. Then you give them the solution. But you don't really give them the solution. You sell them the solution. That's the old style sales letter. And the format actually came out of old school direct mail and it used to work. In fact, there are times where it will still work. But times change and nowadays what happens is people just scroll the bottom of the letter and then they look at what the price is and then they decide if they want to read the letter. Those old style sales letters, they're just not that effective anymore. And their effectiveness, well, it just keeps on dropping. In today's world, we're moving more towards transparency and authenticity and creating value before the sale. And by the way, I think that's a good thing. And that's where the sideways sales letter comes in. This is the big innovation that literally changed online marketing and to a large degree leveled the playing field. So instead of thinking of it as a long 10 page sales letter, thinking about taking it and turning it on its side, think of it as a 10 day process, a 10 day launch where you deliver value over that time before you ask for the sale. What you do is you release three pieces of content, what we call pre-launch content or PLC for short. These days, we generally do that with a video. You release the first video and this first video is all about the journey or the opportunity. It's where you show people they can really have a positive change in their life. They really can learn to play songs on their guitar. They can learn to meditate. They can train their dog. They can live a more healthy lifestyle or they can have a better relationship. You show them the opportunity to change their life for the better. And all along the way, you're teaching, you're giving real value because in this is important, I believe that showing someone that they can change their life for the better is one of the greatest gifts you can give them. Then a few days later, you release a second video, PLC 2. This is the transformation video. That's where you make the transformation real for them. Again, this is a high value video with lots of great content. Oftentimes, you'll have case studies in here and you'll show them how they can really have that transformation. Then a few days later, you release the third video. This one's PLC 3, which is what we call the ownership video. You deliver more great content and then you show them what it's actually like to have that change in their lives. Now, here's the key. You're delivering great content every step of the way. Now, how do you deliver that value and how do you know what to put in your pre-launch videos? Well, remember that old problem, agitate, solve, that they use in the old style sales letters? In the sideways sales letter, we turn that on its side because we actually define a problem at the beginning of the video, of the very first video, and then we give the solution in that video. Then towards the end of the video, we'll define another problem and then we'll solve that problem in our next video. My friend Lisa Sesovich, she calls us the PSPS,

## [21:08 - 24:08]

the problem solution, problem solution, because each solution has the seeds of the next problem or the next challenge. It's the mental triggers. This is the deal. There are certain triggers in all of our brains that create enormous influence in the way we make decisions. These mental triggers exist in every one of us and they've been with us for thousands of years and they'll be with us for thousands more. No matter who you are or where you live in the world, these mental triggers have a huge impact on our decision making. We all respond to these triggers. Now, in my product launch formula coaching program, I go through 20 mental triggers along with the best ways to activate them, but in this video, I'm going to get you started with the first six of them. Now, the first mental trigger is community. Community is a very powerful trigger. If we perceive ourselves as being part of a community, then we'll act in accordance with how we think that people in that community are supposed to act. This is a really powerful trigger and it's important to remember that online communities can be just as powerful as traditional communities. So next is anticipation. This is the idea where we anticipate future events. If you think about how powerful events are like birthdays, graduations, holidays, you'll notice they have a powerful hold on us. Think about the way TV and movies set up the sequel or the next season for a show. They pull you in and leave you anticipating the next episode. Then there's authority. Authority is a super powerful trigger to use in your launches. When we perceive someone as being an authority, we listen to them and they are instantly more influential. Here's an example. Almost any type of uniform, like a police officer or a doctor, well, that uniform creates authority. It's very powerful, but there's many ways to establish authority.

## [24:08 - 27:11]

Reciprocity is another great trigger. That's the idea that if we give something to someone, they're going to feel an obligation to give something back to us in return. If you give a gift to someone, they're going to feel like they want to give a gift back to you. Then there's social proof. This is the idea where if we see a lot of other people doing something, then we're going to be more inclined to give it a try. This is why reviews at Amazon and other places are so powerful, but it's just one way to create social proof. The sixth trigger I have for you today is scarcity. Simply put, if there's less of something, then we want it more. Gold, diamonds, ferraris, whatever, just the fact that there's a fewer of something instantly attracts our attention and gets us more interested. These triggers are everywhere in our lives, but I want to talk about how you can use them in your launch and why they make your marketing so irresistible.
